channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: What the 'Catching the Codfather' podcast found out about a fishing tycoon
item.description: NPR's Adrian Ma speaks with Ian Coss, host of GBH's 'Catching the Codfather' podcast, about fishing tycoon Carlos Rafael of New Bedford, Massachusetts.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Adrian Ma speaks with Ian Coss, host of GBH's 'Catching the Codfather' podcast, about fishing tycoon Carlos Rafael of New Bedford, Massachusetts.
item.dc:creator: Mallory Yu

item.title: ICE agents to be deployed to US airports beginning Monday
item.description: The president says he will send ICE agents to airports starting Monday. NPR's Luke Garrett unpacks what we know about the deployment.
item.content:encoded: The president says he will send ICE agents to airports starting Monday. NPR's Luke Garrett unpacks what we know about the deployment.
item.dc:creator: Adrian Ma

item.title: ICE officers set to deploy to airports as delays mount, border czar Homan confirms
item.description: Border czar Tom Homan says ICE agents will help the Transportation Security Administration "move those lines" while also enforcing immigration law.
item.content:encoded:
Border czar Tom Homan says ICE agents will help the Transportation Security Administration "move those lines" while also enforcing immigration law.

(Image credit: Yuki Iwamura)
item.dc:creator: Luke Garrett

item.title: How one Minnesota school is bouncing back after the ICE surge
item.description: NPR spent time inside a Minnesota school talking with educators, parents, and children as it tries to help kids feel safe again after the ICE surge.
item.content:encoded:
NPR spent time inside a Minnesota school talking with educators, parents, and children as it tries to help kids feel safe again after the ICE surge.

(Image credit: Tim Evans for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Meg Anderson

item.title: Airport security lines are long. Here's what to know if you're flying
item.description: Travel experts say passengers need to be prepared, and patient, amid the government shutdown. Until a deal is reached, officials say airport disruptions and delays could get even worse.
item.content:encoded:
Travel experts say passengers need to be prepared, and patient, amid the government shutdown. Until a deal is reached, officials say airport disruptions and delays could get even worse.

(Image credit: Ronaldo Schemidt)
item.dc:creator: Shannon Bond

item.title: Need some uplift? Life Kit has tips on how to be more optimistic
item.description: Studies have shown optimism promotes longevity, can lead to higher career success and even reduce risk of chronic disease. Life Kit offers tips on how to be more optimistic.
item.content:encoded: Studies have shown optimism promotes longevity, can lead to higher career success and even reduce risk of chronic disease. Life Kit offers tips on how to be more optimistic.
item.dc:creator: Andee Tagle

item.title: Robert Mueller, ex-FBI director who led 2016 Russia inquiry, dies at 81
item.description: Mueller's family told The New York Times in August that he had been diagnosed with Parkinson's disease.
item.content:encoded:
Mueller's family told The New York Times in August that he had been diagnosed with Parkinson's disease.

(Image credit: Carolyn Kaster)
item.dc:creator: Chandelis Duster

item.title: Gen Z's relationship with gambling and the unique vulnerabilities it faces
item.description: The majority of young men in Generation Z are gambling -- on sports, on pop culture events, on politics. They're also experiencing of the downsides of gambling.
item.content:encoded: The majority of young men in Generation Z are gambling -- on sports, on pop culture events, on politics. They're also experiencing of the downsides of gambling.
item.dc:creator: Scott Simon

item.title: Iran war enters its fourth week with no clear end in sight
item.description: As the war in the Middle East enters its fourth week, President Trump says the U.S. is considering "winding down" military efforts, as it also seeks to ease the energy crisis by lifting sanctions on Iranian oil stranded at sea.
item.content:encoded:
As the war in the Middle East enters its fourth week, President Trump says the U.S. is considering "winding down" military efforts, as it also seeks to ease the energy crisis by lifting sanctions on Iranian oil stranded at sea.

(Image credit: Amir Levy)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: Jury finds Elon Musk misled investors during Twitter purchase
item.description: A jury has found Elon Musk liable for misleading investors by deliberately driving down Twitter's stock price in the tumultuous months leading up to his 2022 acquisition of the social media company for $44 billion. But it absolved him of some fraud allegations.
item.content:encoded:
A jury has found Elon Musk liable for misleading investors by deliberately driving down Twitter's stock price in the tumultuous months leading up to his 2022 acquisition of the social media company for $44 billion. But it absolved him of some fraud allegations.

(Image credit: Markus Schreiber)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
